explanation: Hebrews 11:1 - Faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.
options: The assurance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen

content: According to Hebrews 11:6, without faith it is impossible to do what?
explanation: Hebrews 11:6 - Without faith it is impossible to please Him. Whoever comes to God must believe that He exists and rewards those who seek Him.

content: Hebrews 12:2 urges us to run the race by looking unto whom?
explanation: Hebrews 12:2 - Fixing our eyes on Jesus, the author and finisher of our faith.
options: Jesus, the author and finisher of faith

content: How does Hebrews 4:12 describe the Word of God?
explanation: The Word of God is living and active, piercing the heart, discerning thoughts and intentions.
options: Living and powerful, sharper than any two-edged sword

content: Hebrews 4:15: In what way is Jesus our High Priest?
explanation: Jesus understands our weaknesses because He has gone through everything. Therefore, let us boldly approach the throne of grace.
options: Tempted in every way as we are, yet without sin

content: Hebrews 11:8: What did Abraham do by faith when he was called?
explanation: Faith is obedience even when the destination is unseen. Abraham is a model of faith.
options: Obeyed and went without knowing where he was going

content: Hebrews 12:6: Whom does the Lord discipline?
explanation: Discipline is a sign of the Father's love, not abandonment.

content: Hebrews 13:5: What does the Lord promise about His presence?
explanation: The Lord is always with us. He will never forsake us. Therefore, be content with what you have.
options: I will never leave you nor forsake you

content: Hebrews 13:8: What is Jesus Christ yesterday, today, and forever?
explanation: Jesus is unchanging. He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

content: Hebrews 9:22: According to the law, without ___ there is no forgiveness of sins
explanation: Core principle: forgiveness of sins requires blood. The blood of Jesus is the final and most perfect sacrifice.

content: Hebrews 11:3: By faith we understand that the world was created by what?
explanation: Faith accepts that God created the universe by His Word out of nothing.

content: Hebrews 10:23: Hold fast the confession of hope, for He who promised is what?
explanation: God is faithful to keep His promises, so we can steadfastly confess our faith.
